topics: Research and Explain Three Real-World IoT Applications Using the Components Available in the IoT Trainer Kit
Set up the IoT Trainer Kit and Understand Its Modules
Use the Temperature and Humidity Sensor Module in the IoT Trainer Kit to Collect Environmental Data and Display the Readings on the Provided Interface
Interface the Light and Sound Sensors from the IoT Trainer Kit, Capture Data, and Trigger Actions Such as Lighting an LED When the Sound Level Exceeds a Threshold
Set up an MQTT Communication System Using the IoT Trainer Kit, Publish Sensor Data to a Topic, and Subscribe to Another Topic to Receive Control Signals
Send Sensor Data to a Local Server via HTTP Using the IoT Trainer Kit and Create a Simple Web Interface to Display the Data
Control an LED and Relay Module in the IoT Trainer Kit, Implementing a Scenario Where the Relay Controls a Fan Based on Temperature Readings
Utilize the PWM Functionality in the IoT Trainer Kit to Control the Speed of a Motor, Adjusting Motor Speed Based on Sensor Input Such as Light Intensity
Configure the IoT Trainer Kit to Send Sensor Data to a Local Server for Storage and Visualization, Using Basic Analytics to Process the Data
Write a Script to Analyze Sensor Data from the IoT Trainer Kit, Such as Calculating Averages or Identifying Trends, and Visualize the Results
Implement Basic Encryption Techniques Using the IoT Trainer Kit to Secure Data Transmission and Demonstrate How Data Can Be Encrypted and Decrypted
Project: Design and Implement a Project That Uses Multiple Components of the IoT Trainer Kit to Create a Functional IoT System, Such as a Smart Home System
